title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence · Go Semantics Case: Pokedex Go Catch Product Rule
summary: Catch uses a hidden global random source, stores partial response pointers, duplicates the same Pokemon unpredictably, and makes tests depend on luck and request timing. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go catch product rule increment in deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Define whether catch is guaranteed, probabilistic, skill-based, or a collection action and state duplicate and failure semantics.
Verify request identity, response identity, source URL, cache state, unit conversion, sorting, missing fields, and changed fixtures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
